github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text: Write commits in Conventional Commits v1.0.0 format: `<type>(<scope>)!?: <subject>`. The scope and the `!` breaking-change marker are optional.
Allowed types (use exactly these, lower-case): feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert. Pick the single best fit; do not invent new types.
Subject rules: imperative mood (`add X`, not `added X` or `adds X`), lower-case first letter, no trailing period, concrete and specific. Describe the change, not the file touched.
Hard limit: the subject line (type + scope + subject) must be 115 characters or fewer. Aim for under 72 when the change is simple; use the extra headroom only when it genuinely helps.
Scope is optional. If used, keep it short (one word, an area like `config`, `ci`, `gui`, `cli`, or a tracker acronym like `bt`, `asc`). Uppercase tracker acronyms are acceptable.
Body (optional, separated from subject by a blank line): explain *why* the change was made and any non-obvious context. Wrap body lines at ~115 characters. Do not restate what the diff already shows.
Footer (optional, separated from body by a blank line): use for `BREAKING CHANGE: <desc>`, `Closes: #123`, `Refs: #456`, `Co-authored-by: Name <email>`. Wrap footer lines at ~115 characters.
Breaking changes: either append `!` after the type/scope (e.g. `feat(api)!: drop v1 endpoints`) or add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer explaining the impact. Both is fine.

Examples that pass the repo's commit-message validator: `feat(config): add YAML importer`, `fix(bt): correct duplicate search payload`, `chore(ci): switch to pnpm action v5`, `feat!: drop Go 1.19 support`, `revert: undo e3f2a1b`.

github.copilot.chat.pullRequestDescripti.text: PR title must be a valid Conventional Commit (same rules as commit messages: type + optional scope + imperative subject, 115 chars max). The PR title becomes the squashed commit message.
PR description: start with a short Summary (1–3 bullets of *what* and *why*), then a Test plan checklist, then any screenshots or links. Wrap prose at ~115 characters.
